Global (All).title: Rising tensions between US and China over Taiwan
Global (All).summary: The US and China have stepped up their rhetoric against each other over Taiwan, with the US promising to support Taiwan's defense and China vowing to take all necessary measures to prevent its independence.

Global (All).title: COVID-19 cases surge in several countries
Global (All).summary: Several countries have reported a surge in COVID-19 cases, causing concerns about the effectiveness of vaccination programs and the potential for new variants to spread.

Global (All).title: Protests and unrest in several African cities
Global (All).summary: Protests and unrest have broken out in several cities across Africa, caused by issues such as poverty, corruption, and economic hardship.

United States.title: Multiple Fatalities in Highland Park July 4th Parade Shooting
United States.summary: A gunman opened fire on a Fourth of July parade in a Chicago suburb, killing at least six people and wounding dozens more. The suspect fled the scene and was later arrested after a manhunt.

United States.title: Supreme Court Upholds Biden Administration's Student Loan Forgiveness Plan
United States.summary: The Supreme Court ruled in a 5-4 decision that the Biden administration's student loan forgiveness plan is constitutional, rejecting a challenge from six Republican-led states.

United States.title: TSA Extends Mask Mandate for Domestic Flights Despite CDC Recommendation
United States.summary: The Transportation Security Administration (TSA) announced that it will continue to require masks on domestic flights, despite the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) recommending lifting the mandate.

South Korea.title: North Korea Missile Test
South Korea.summary: North Korea launched an intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) into the Sea of Japan on July 4, 2023, raising concerns about its nuclear program and South Korea's national security.

South Korea.title: South Korea's Presidential Election Uncertainty
South Korea.summary: The upcoming presidential election in South Korea on March 9, 2024, is creating uncertainty about the future direction of the country's politics, economy, and security.

South Korea.title: COVID-19 Outbreak in South Korea's Military
South Korea.summary: A COVID-19 outbreak in South Korea's military has raised concerns about the country's military readiness and public health.

Japan.event_id1: Japanese Prime Minister's approval rating falls to lowest since 2022
Japan.summary1: 'Fumio Kishida's approval rating has fallen, amid concerns over the ruling Liberal Democratic Party's management of the economy.

Japan.event_id2: Tokyo and Beijing engage in a new round of diplomatic tension
Japan.summary2: 'The diplomatic standoff between China and Japan has intensified, amid a visit by a Japanese official to Taiwan, prompting protests in China.

Japan.event_id3: Concerns mount over Japan's ageing population
Japan.summary3: 'The country's population is shrinking rapidly, with serious implications for its tax base and pension system.
